items.title: SonicWall SMA appliances targeted in zero-day attacks (CVE-2026-15409, CVE-2026-15410)
items.description: SonicWall has fixed two actively exploited vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-15409, CVE-2026-15410) affecting its Secure Mobile Access (SMA) 1000 Series appliances, and is urging customer organizations to upgrade to a fixed firmare version and search for evidence of potential compromise. If the outlined indicators of compromise are present on the system, the company advises re-imaging (hardware) or re-deploying (virtual) appliances, changing user and administrator passwords, and resetting TOTP tokens. T

items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-15409 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-14T21:17:22Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2026-15409", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc

items.title: Fedify has an incomplete SSRF mitigation after GHSA-p9cg-vqcc-grcx: validatePublicUrl allows special-use IPv4 ranges
items.description:
### Summary

Fedify previously addressed SSRF/internal network access in GHSA-p9cg-vqcc-grcx by adding public URL validation before runtime document and media fetching. However, the current IPv4 validation logic appears incomplete.

The `validatePublicUrl()` protection relies on `isValidPublicIPv4Address()` to reject non-public IPv4 destinations. The function blocks common private and local ranges such as `10.0.0.0/8`, `127.0.0.0/8`, `169.254.0.0/16`, `172.16.0.0/12`, and `192.168.0.0/16`, but it still treats several special-use, reserved, multicast, benchmarking, and carrier-grade NAT IPv4 ranges as valid public destinations.

Because this validation is used as an SSRF defense before outbound fetches, this appears to be an incomplete mitigation or bypass class for the previous SSRF issue.

items.description: Missing Authorization vulnerability in WPXPO WowAddons product-addons allows Exploiting Incorrectly Configured Access Control Security Levels.This issue affects WowAddons: from n/a through <= 1.6.8.
items.apex_ai_summary: [MEDIUM] CVE advisory. Risk score 6.5/10. Full APEX AI analysis available to Pro subscribers.

apex.ai_summary: [P2] Vulnerability - moderate severity (Risk 6.5/10). No MITRE ATT&CK techniques mapped - limited adversarial context. 2 indicators detected - upgrade to Pro for full IOC access. AI confidence: LOW (22%) - limited source data available.
apex.recommended_action: HIGH PRIORITY - Patch within 72 hours. Monitor affected assets. Review threat hunting queries for related TTPs.

ioc_paywall.message: 2 IOC(s) at 16.0% confidence - unlock with Pro tier.

items.nvd_disclosure: This CVE ID was not found in the NIST NVD database at time of enrichment. Intelligence is preliminary and unverified by NVD. CVSS score and severity are analyst-estimated until NVD confirmation.
items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-57377 Missing Authorization vulnerability in WPXPO WowAddons product-ad
id: 431746cd-f441-5ca8-bf7b-6f73e68dfa85
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-57377 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: MEDIUM | Class: GENERIC
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-57377
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/14
modified: 2026/07/14
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity
level: medium
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-57377 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-14T21:17:22Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2026-57377", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2026-57377 Exploitation Attempt - GENERIC"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|90 90 90 90|"; rawbytes; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:6662961; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-14, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
items.confidence_rationale: Medium confidence — Source 'nvd_cve' is authoritative (government/standards body)
items.recommended_sla_action: SCHEDULED PATCH — Apply in next maintenance window

items.title: U.S. CISA adds a Cisco IOS flaw to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog
items.description: U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) adds a Cisco IOS flaw to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) added a Cisco IOS flaw, tracked as CVE-2008-4128, to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog. Cisco IOS 12.4 running on Cisco 871 Integrated Services Routers contains multiple CSRF flaws in […]
